topic: The Lyceum Greenfield Architecture — Wednesday Feb 18
summary: The Lyceum greenfield architecture defined: Next.js + Supabase + Stripe, targeting premium faith-integrated learning community.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning everyone. This is a special session — we're here to architect The Lyceum from scratch. Let me be clear on scope: this is a greenfield project. No Lever app dependencies, no joshlevylabs.com, no Clawdbot Hub. Clean slate. We need to answer three questions today: Who is this for? What's the MVP? And what do we build it on? Let's start with Dave on the market side.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: The Lyceum — the name itself signals something. It's Aristotle's school, it's a place of learning and discourse. I think we're looking at a premium knowledge community, likely centered around faith-integrated personal development given Josh's content expertise. Target user is a motivated professional, probably 28-45, who wants structured growth frameworks but with a spiritual backbone. The monetization path I see is a cohort-based or subscription community — think $47-$97/month — with a free tier that demonstrates value. We need Josh to confirm the audience and pricing philosophy before we wire anything up.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Dave's right on the name resonance, but I want to push the vision further. The Lyceum should be built around what I call a "value-first funnel" — the free experience has to be so good that people feel compelled to go deeper. I'm thinking the MVP content pillars are daily lessons, a structured curriculum track, and some form of community interaction. The brand needs to feel like a private library meets a mastermind — exclusive but welcoming. For launch, we need a landing page that collects emails before anything else ships. I can draft brand guidelines and a positioning doc once Josh picks the audience direction. Elon (CTO): Alright, let's talk architecture. Greenfield means we get to do this right. My recommendation: Next.js 14 with App Router on Vercel for the frontend and hosting — it's fast, it scales, and deployment is zero-friction. Supabase for the backend — gives us Postgres, auth, row-level security, and real-time subscriptions out of the box. For payments, Stripe with a simple subscription model. Content management through MDX files in the repo initially — no CMS overhead until we actually need it. The MVP build is tight: auth, a lesson feed, a curriculum track with progress, and a Stripe-gated premium tier. I can have a scaffolded repo with auth and basic routing in 48 hours. One critical decision for Josh: do we want community features like discussion or comments in MVP, or do we ship content-first and layer community in v2?

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Let's do cross-talk. Elon, Dave mentioned cohort-based — does that change anything in the architecture? Elon (CTO): It changes the data model slightly but not the stack. Cohorts just mean we add a time-gated content release system and group users into enrollment windows. Supabase handles that cleanly with row-level policies. I'd say design for cohorts from day one in the schema even if we launch with evergreen access — it's cheap to build in now and expensive to retrofit.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: I agree with Elon on that. And Alex, on the free tier — I want to be specific. I'd recommend the first 3-5 lessons free, then a hard paywall. Not a soft gate, not "donate what you want." Clear value exchange. We can A/B test the gate placement later but we need revenue signal from day one.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Agreed on the hard gate. The free lessons need to be the best content in the product though — that's the offer. They should create an open loop that makes the paid content feel inevitable. I'll structure the content arc with that psychology in mind once Josh decides on the curriculum topic.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Let me close this out. Three priorities are clear. First, Josh needs to make the foundational product decisions — target audience, content focus, and whether community is in or out of MVP. Second, Elon scaffolds the greenfield repo on Next.js plus Supabase with auth, lesson feed, progress tracking, and Stripe integration. Third, Alex and Dave collaborate on the launch positioning — brand guidelines, landing page copy, and pricing structure — pending Josh's direction. This is a clean, focused build. Let's not scope-creep it. Here are the action items.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Alright team, Josh has made three decisive calls and they reshape this project in interesting ways. First, The Lyceum isn't a general personal development community — it's a technical education platform for R&D and manufacturing engineers building electronics. That's a very specific, high-value niche, and it means the curriculum, the brand voice, and the positioning all need to speak to engineers, not general professionals. Dave, Alex — your earlier assumptions

actionItems.text: Decide target audience and content focus for The Lyceum
Decide whether community features are in MVP or deferred to v2
Choose pricing model and free tier scope
Scaffold greenfield Lyceum repo: Next.js 14 App Router, Supabase auth, Postgres schema with cohort-ready lesson model, Stripe subscription integration
Draft brand positioning document and landing page copy framework for The Lyceum
Draft pricing and monetization strategy document with free-to-paid conversion funnel
Design Lyceum data model: users, lessons, curriculum tracks, progress, cohorts, subscriptions

topPriorities: Joshua decides target audience, content focus, and community-in-MVP vs content-only MVP
Scaffold greenfield repo with Next.js 14, Supabase auth, lesson feed, progress tracking, and Stripe
Draft brand positioning, landing page copy, and pricing structure once Joshua sets direction

ceoDecisions.question: Who is The Lyceum's primary target user?
ceoDecisions.answer: CUSTOM: R&D and manufacturing engineers at tech companies building electronics
ceoDecisions.answerText: R&D and manufacturing engineers at tech companies building electronics
options: Faith-driven professionals seeking structured personal development
Church leaders and ministry workers wanting teaching frameworks
General self-improvement audience with optional faith integration

ceoDecisions.question: Should community features (discussion, comments, peer interaction) be included in the MVP?
ceoDecisions.answerText: No — ship content-only MVP, add community in v2
options: Yes — lightweight discussion threads in MVP
No — ship content-only MVP, add community in v2
Include a private Discord/Circle link as interim community

ceoDecisions.question: What is the pricing model for launch?
options: Free tier + $197 one-time course purchase model
